I know that some divers can last up to fifty-seven seconds, and highly skillful ones to eighty-seven; but such men are rare, and when the poor fellows climb back on board, the water coming out of their noses and ears is tinted with blood. I believe the average time underwater that these fishermen can tolerate is thirty seconds, during which they hastily stuff their little nets with all the pearl oysters they can tear loose. But these fishermen generally don't live to advanced age: their vision weakens, ulcers break out on their eyes, sores form on their bodies, and some are even stricken with apoplexy on the ocean floor."

From its commencement the Amazon is recognizable as destined to become a magnificent stream. There are neither rapids nor obstacles of any sort until it reaches a defile where its course is slightly narrowed between two picturesque and unequal precipices. No falls are met with until this point is reached, where it curves to the eastward, and passes through the intermediary chain of the Andes. Hereabouts are a few waterfalls, were it not for which the river would be navigable from its mouth to its source. description: The great mountain wall of central Europe, giant of the continent crowned by Mont Blanc, a measure against which Verne weighs all other heights.
Havre is the Channel port whose passengers embark in [[The Survivors of the Chancellor]]. (SC2-002-006) Its shipping crowds the sea-lanes of [[Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea]]. (TTLU-042-052)
tags: [place, pacific, exploration]

Through Bridgeport's submarine boat The Protector it prefigures the amphibious ambitions of the Terror in *The Master of the World*, while the 1882 tempest ties it to the meteorological wonders of *Robur the Conqueror*. It stands among the New England coastal states — beside Massachusetts and Maine — that recur along the seaboard of Verne's American scenes.
description: 'Historic region of central Africa crossed by the balloon Victoria and a recurring frontier of Vernean exploration.'
aliases: [State of Maryland]
It stands among the scattered survivors of the old Mediterranean world — Gourbi Island, Gibraltar, and Ceuta — none of which was harmed at the first concussion. (OC-042-003)

Lan-Tcheou marks a bend in the Great Wall: after "descending to the southeast toward Lan-Tcheou," the wall turns to the northeast, covering the provinces north of Pekin. (ASC-022-072) On leaving the city the railway "crosses a well-cultivated country, watered by numerous streams, and hilly enough to necessitate frequent curves." (ASC-024-001)

Once the last of the ice-wall had dissolved the island lay stationary, all the force of the current running deep below the waves rather than on the surface (FC-044-002). Of the hundred and forty square miles that had once formed the island, only twenty at length remained (FC-045-016).
- [[Ivan Ogareff]] — the traitor who uses her to expose Michael's identity (MS-023-017).
- [[Michel Strogoff]] — the courier whose features she fixes in memory and helps to unmask (MS-009-012).
